heading: Blue Economy valuation
label: 3613 Blue Economy valuation
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, and not as part of the Integrated Coastal and Ocean Observation System Act of 2009 which comprises this chapter.
DefinitionsFor definitions of “Administrator”, “Administration”, and “Indian Tribe” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 10102 of Pub. L. 117–263section 3612 of this title

children.heading: Measurement of industries
children.label: a Measurement of industries
children.text: The Administrator, in consultation with the heads of other relevant Federal agencies and subject to the availability of appropriations, shall establish a program to improve the collection, aggregation, and analysis of data to measure the value and impact of industries related to the Great Lakes, oceans, bays, estuaries, and coasts on the economy of the United States, including military uses, living resources, marine construction, marine transportation, offshore energy development and siting including for renewable energy, offshore mineral production, ship and boat building, tourism, recreation, subsistence, commercial, recreational, and charter fishing, seafood processing, and other fishery-related businesses, aquaculture such as kelp and shellfish, and other industries the Administrator determines appropriate.

work with the Director of the Bureau of Economic Analysis and the heads of other relevant Federal agencies to develop a Coastal and Ocean Economy Satellite Account that includes national, Tribal, and State-level statistics to measure the contribution of the Great Lakes, oceans, bays, estuaries, and coasts to the overall economy of the United States; and
collaborate with national and international organizations and governments to promote consistency of methods, measurements, and definitions to ensure comparability of results between countries.
defines the Blue Economy in consultation with Indian Tribes and with input from academia, the private sector, nongovernmental organizations, and other relevant experts;
makes recommendations for updating North American Industry Classification System reporting codes to reflect the Blue Economy; and

children.label: i
children.text: economic activities that are dependent upon the resources of the Great Lakes, oceans, bays, estuaries, and coasts;

children.label: ii
children.text: the population and demographic characteristics of the population along the coasts;

children.label: iii
children.text: port and shoreline infrastructure;

children.label: iv
children.text: the volume and value of cargo shipped by sea or across the Great Lakes;

children.label: v
children.text: data collected from the Great Lakes, oceans, bays, estuaries, and coasts, including such data collected by businesses that purchase and commodify the data, including weather prediction and seasonal agricultural forecasting; and

children.label: vi
children.text: military uses; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent possible, the qualified value and impact of the natural capital of the Great Lakes, oceans, bays, estuaries, and coasts with respect to tourism, recreation, natural resources, and cultural heritage, including other indirect values.

children.heading: Centralized website for resiliency grants
children.label: d Centralized website for resiliency grants

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Administrator, in coordination with the heads of other relevant Federal agencies, shall create and regularly update a public website to improve education, outreach, and public information regarding grants and other public funding opportunities of the Administration related to resiliency.December 23, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: the Administration;

children.label: ii
children.text: other relevant Federal agencies; or

children.label: iii
children.text: foundations in coordination with the Administration.

children.label: B
children.text: Information described in subparagraph (A) that is specific to supporting Tribal Governments and Tribal Colleges and Universities, and, with respect to each such grant described in paragraph (1), the contact information for an individual of the Administration who can assist Tribal Governments and Tribal Colleges and Universities in applying for such grants.

children.label: C
children.text: Information described in subparagraph (A) that is specific to supporting Historically Black Colleges and Universities, and, with respect to each such grant described in paragraph (1), the contact information for an individual of the Administration who can assist Historically Black Colleges and Universities in applying for such grants.

children.heading: Outreach
children.label: 3 Outreach
children.text: The Administrator shall conduct outreach activities to inform State, Tribal, and local governments of resiliency, adaptation, and mitigation grants that are available to such governments.

children.heading: Historically Black Colleges and Universities
children.label: A Historically Black Colleges and Universities
children.text: The term “Historically Black Colleges and Universities” has the meaning given the term “part B institution” in .section 1061 of title 20

children.heading: Tribal College or University
children.label: B Tribal College or University
children.text: The term “Tribal College or University” has the meaning given the term in .section 1059c of title 20
